question.text: Under Regulation 31(1) of SEBI LODR 2015, the shareholding pattern must be filed with stock exchanges within how many days from the end of each quarter?

answer.text: Within 30 days of the quarter end
Within 21 days of the quarter end
Within 45 days of the quarter end
Within 15 days of the quarter end

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 31(1) mandates that the shareholding pattern must be filed within 21 days from the end of each quarter. Additionally, it must be filed 1 day before listing and within 10 days of any capital restructuring resulting in a change of more than 2% in shareholding. **The Trap:** 30 days is the deadline for investor complaints (Reg 13), not shareholding pattern. The 21-day deadline is shorter because shareholding data is critical for market transparency. **Cross-Exam Context:** RTAs play a key role in preparing shareholding pattern data from their records. NISM Series IX also covers this filing requirement from the merchant banker's perspective. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 21-day deadline is the tightest among quarterly LODR filings. RTAs must have systems to compile beneficial owner data from depositories quickly after quarter-end to meet this deadline.
